req.title: Overdraft protection (idempotency variant)
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-087 defines four distinct decision branches: (1) overdraft covered by linked savings, (2) overdraft exceeds linked savings so transaction is declined, (3) debit amount exactly equals linked savings balance (boundary), and (4) duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key returns original result (idempotency). EP partitions the input space into covered vs. uncovered overdraft amounts. BVA targets the exact-balance edge. STC models the state machine from PENDING -> APPROVED/DECLINED. DT captures all precondition combinations (checking insufficient, savings sufficient/insufficient, key reused). NEG validates the decline pathway. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that seed and tear down account state, and uses a unique Idempotency-Key generated from a UUID to remain deterministic and idempotent across runs.

tests.name: test_overdraft_covered_by_linked_savings
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with savings_balance=200.00
Overdraft link is active between checking and savings
actions: POST /overdraft with amount=100.00, unique Idempotency-Key, checking_account_id, savings_account_id
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-087: linked savings debited when debit exceeds checking balance
R-FIN-087: fee assessed on successful overdraft draw
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, seed_accounts, teardown_accounts

def test_overdraft_covered_by_linked_savings(api_client, seed_accounts):
    ctx = seed_accounts(
        checking_balance=50.00,
        savings_balance=200.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": ctx["checking_id"],
        "savings_account_id": ctx["savings_id"],
        "amount": 100.00
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "APPROVED"
    assert body["overdraft_draw"] == pytest.approx(50.00)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] == pytest.approx(10.00)
    assert "transaction_id" in body
    accts = <helper_fn>.get_balances(ctx)
    assert accts["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(150.00)
    teardown_accounts(ctx)

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with savings_balance=30.00
Overdraft link is active between checking and savings
Requested debit amount would require a draw of 80.00 from savings
actions: POST /overdraft with amount=100.00, unique Idempotency-Key, checking_account_id, savings_account_id
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity or 402 Payment Required
no debit applied to either account
trace: R-FIN-087: transaction declined when linked savings balance insufficient
R-FIN-087: error pathway - declined when linked balance insufficient
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, seed_accounts, teardown_accounts

def test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient(api_client, seed_accounts):
    ctx = seed_accounts(
        checking_balance=20.00,
        savings_balance=30.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": ctx["checking_id"],
        "savings_account_id": ctx["savings_id"],
        "amount": 100.00
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (402, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "DECLINED"
    assert body["error_code"] == "INSUFFICIENT_LINKED_BALANCE"
    accts = <helper_fn>.get_balances(ctx)
    assert accts["checking_balance"] == pytest.approx(20.00)
    assert accts["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(30.00)
    assert body.get("overdraft_fee", 0) == 0
    teardown_accounts(ctx)

tests.name: test_overdraft_draw_equals_exact_savings_balance
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with savings_balance=100.00
Requested debit amount equals exactly checking_balance + savings_balance
actions: POST /overdraft with amount=100.00 (requires full savings draw), unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: savings account balance reduced to exactly 0.00
trace: R-FIN-087: draw up to linked account available balance - boundary at exact balance
BVA: draw amount == savings_balance is the maximum valid boundary
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, seed_accounts, teardown_accounts

def test_overdraft_draw_equals_exact_savings_balance(api_client, seed_accounts):
    ctx = seed_accounts(
        checking_balance=0.00,
        savings_balance=100.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": ctx["checking_id"],
        "savings_account_id": ctx["savings_id"],
        "amount": 100.00
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "APPROVED"
    assert body["overdraft_draw"] == pytest.approx(100.00)
    accts = <helper_fn>.get_balances(ctx)
    assert accts["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
    teardown_accounts(ctx)

tests.name: test_overdraft_idempotent_duplicate_submission
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with savings_balance=200.00
A prior successful overdraft request was made with Idempotency-Key=KEY-DUPE-001
actions: POST /overdraft first time with amount=100.00 and Idempotency-Key=KEY-DUPE-001
Record first response body and transaction_id
POST /overdraft second time with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key=KEY-DUPE-001
expected: Both response bodies are identical (same transaction_id, status, overdraft_draw, fee)
Savings account debited only once (not twice)
trace: R-FIN-087: idempotency - duplicate Idempotency-Key returns original result
DT: precondition combination - same key, same payload, repeated submission
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, seed_accounts, teardown_accounts

def test_overdraft_idempotent_duplicate_submission(api_client, seed_accounts):
    ctx = seed_accounts(
        checking_balance=50.00,
        savings_balance=200.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    idem_key = "KEY-DUPE-R-FIN-087-" + ctx["test_run_id"]
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": ctx["checking_id"],
        "savings_account_id": ctx["savings_id"],
        "amount": 100.00
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    resp1 = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    body1 = resp1.json()
    resp2 = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body1["transaction_id"] == body2["transaction_id"]
    assert body1["status"] == body2["status"] == "APPROVED"
    assert body1["overdraft_draw"] == body2["overdraft_draw"]
    assert body1["overdraft_fee"] == body2["overdraft_fee"]
    accts = <helper_fn>.get_balances(ctx)
    assert accts["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(150.00)
    teardown_accounts(ctx)

tests.name: test_overdraft_state_transition_pending_to_approved_to_settled
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with savings_balance=150.00
actions: POST /overdraft with amount=60.00 and unique Idempotency-Key
GET /overdraft/{transaction_id} immediately to capture PENDING or APPROVED state
Poll /overdraft/{transaction_id} until status reaches SETTLED or terminal state
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 200 with status APPROVED or PENDING
Subsequent GET shows state progression: PENDING -> APPROVED -> SETTLED
Final state is SETTLED with correct overdraft_draw and fee
trace: R-FIN-087: overdraft operation lifecycle - state transition coverage
STC: models PENDING -> APPROVED -> SETTLED path for valid overdraft
tests.executable:
import uuid
import time
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, seed_accounts, teardown_accounts

VALID_TRANSITIONS = {"PENDING": {"APPROVED", "DECLINED"}, "APPROVED": {"SETTLED"}}

def test_overdraft_state_transition_pending_to_approved_to_settled(api_client, seed_accounts):
    ctx = seed_accounts(
        checking_balance=10.00,
        savings_balance=150.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": ctx["checking_id"],
        "savings_account_id": ctx["savings_id"],
        "amount": 60.00
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers={"Idempotency-Key": idem_key})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    txn_id = resp.json()["transaction_id"]
    prev_status = resp.json()["status"]
    assert prev_status in ("PENDING", "APPROVED")
    for _ in range(10):
        time.sleep(1)
        poll = api_client.get(f"/overdraft/{txn_id}")
        curr = poll.json()["status"]
        if prev_status in VALID_TRANSITIONS:
            assert curr in VALID_TRANSITIONS[prev_status] or curr == prev_status
        prev_status = curr
        if curr == "SETTLED":
            break
    assert prev_status == "SETTLED"
    teardown_accounts(ctx)
